steps.title: Use the date condition
steps.content: The sentence asks for examples of hoards discovered before 2000. The completing choice must name hoards with discovery dates earlier than 2000.

steps.title: Check the dates
steps.content: Choice B is vague because it mentions the 1800s without giving the requested specific examples from the data. Choice C says both hoards were discovered in the 2000s, which is after the cutoff. Choice D includes 2013, also after 2000, and misassigns the 1940 date. The remaining choice gives two pre-2000 dates. The answer is A.
